items.text:
Subject: Career Fair RSVP Confirmed — Booth Map Attached

Hi Maya,

Thanks for registering for the Spring Career Fair on March 14, from 10:00 AM to 3:00 PM in the Whitman Student Center main hall. Your $5 student entry fee has been received, and you're checked in for the early-access slot from 9:30 AM. Please bring a printed resume and your campus ID for the door scanners.

The attached booth map shows that engineering recruiters are clustered along the north wall, and nonprofit organizations along the south wall. Plan ahead — the most popular booths often have lines by 10:30 AM. Visitors who arrive without a confirmation email cannot enter the early-access window.

If you can no longer attend, please email Ms. Park at careers@whitman.edu by March 10 to free up your slot for another student. Otherwise, we'll see you on the 14th.

Best regards,
Whitman Career Services

format_metadata.subject: Career Fair RSVP Confirmed — Booth Map Attached

options.A: A printed resume and her campus ID
options.B: A signed copy of the booth map
options.C: A receipt for her registration fee
options.D: A list of recruiter contact details

questions.explanation: The email says Maya should bring a printed resume and her campus ID for the door scanners.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To recruit volunteers for the Spring Career Fair
options.B: To explain a new dress code for career events
options.C: To confirm registration and share key fair details
options.D: To announce the closure of careers@whitman.edu

questions.explanation: The email confirms Maya's registration and provides time, location, fees, and entry instructions for the fair.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students who arrive at 11:00 AM?

options.A: Their resumes will not be reviewed at all
options.B: Their entry fee will be refunded by Career Services
options.C: Their booth assignments will likely be changed
options.D: Their wait at popular booths is likely to be longer

questions.explanation: Because popular booths often have lines by 10:30 AM, students arriving later are likely to face longer waits.

items.text:
Subject: Welcome to the Greenfield Makerspace

Dear Dani,

Welcome to the Greenfield Campus Makerspace! Your membership is active starting February 1 and runs through May 20. The shop is open Monday through Friday, 9:00 AM to 8:00 PM, and Saturday 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM in Room 217 of Lockwood Hall.

Before using any equipment, you must complete the online safety module in the member portal and attend one in-person orientation. The next orientation runs February 6 at 5:00 PM, led by shop manager Mr. Ortiz. Plan to stay for the full hour, since the second half covers the laser cutter and the 3D printers.

Projects can be left on labeled shelves for up to two weeks; any material left longer will be recycled. If you have questions about specific tools, reach out to makerspace@greenfield.edu before stopping by.

Thanks,
Greenfield Makerspace Team

questions.stem: According to the email, what must Dani do before using equipment?

options.A: Schedule a private session with Mr. Ortiz
options.B: Finish the online safety module and attend orientation
options.C: Pay a separate fee for the laser cutter access
options.D: Submit a project proposal through the member portal

questions.explanation: The email says members must complete the online safety module and attend one in-person orientation before using equipment.

questions.stem: What is the main purpose of this email?

options.A: To advertise an upcoming campus 3D-printing competition
options.B: To welcome a new member and explain how to start using the shop
options.C: To announce a new contact for the makerspace this semester
options.D: To remind members about an upcoming move to Lockwood Hall

questions.explanation: The email welcomes Dani and explains hours, location, required training, and storage rules for getting started.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about projects left longer than two weeks?

options.A: They will be moved to an off-site storage room
options.B: They will be discarded and the materials reused
options.C: They will be returned to the owner by Mr. Ortiz
options.D: They will be displayed in the campus gallery

questions.explanation: The email states that any material left longer than two weeks will be recycled, implying the project is discarded and materials reused.
